a. Identify the reconciling items of reciprocal accounts Due to/from Other Funds and prepare necessary adjustments in the books; and

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality conduct a complete physical inventory count of all its properties, plant and equipment and submit the required RPCPPE to the auditor’s office pursuant to the requirement of Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 490 of GAAM, Volume I.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the appropriation for Personal Services for one fiscal year shall not exceed the required 45% of the total annual income from regular sources realized by the Municipality in the next preceding fiscal year pursuant to Section 325(a) of R.A. 7160.
Also, the Municipality should exert effort by raising revenues or reducing its PS expenses so that it could implement all planned projects and provide additional PS costs. Otherwise, maximize the services of existing employees and minimize the hiring of casual and job order personnel.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Accountant:
a. Require the Accountable Officers to liquidate their outstanding cash advances, through submission of the necessary supporting documents and refund the unexpended balance, if any.
b. Immediately send demand letters to officials and employees who have long outstanding cash advances to settle the overdue amounts within a specific period. Otherwise, effect the withholding of their salaries pursuant to 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 until such time that the cash advances are fully settled.
c. Strictly require officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed periods pursuant to 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
d. Immediately send a follow up letter to COA – ICFAU to ascertain the status of previously submitted liquidation documents for charges against previous years’ confidential fund and request for the issuance of credit advice or credit notice.
e. File request for authority to write-off dormant or long outstanding unliquidated cash advances, if necessary, to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit the following documents in order to validate that the Municipality employees have met all the requirements warranting the grant of PIB:
a. Evaluation reports or any documentation recognizing suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other personal efforts which resulted in monetary savings making the employees eligible for PRAISE award; and
b. Report detailing the savings generated during CY 2017 which prompted the grant of PIB and relevant supporting documents.
We also recommend that the Municipality submit specific legal basis for the grant of PIB in the event that the above documents could not be submitted.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality and the MAGE:
a. Submit subsisting CNA which includes a provision on cost-cutting measures and systems improvement approaches which the management and the union are bound to collectively undertake pursuant to the requirement of Item 4.1.1 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2017-3 dated November 16, 2017.
b. Submit evidential documents to prove that the Municipality generated savings from the allowable MOOE items as identified under Item. 4.3.6 of the aforementioned Budget Circular.
c. Submit accomplishment reports of PPAs programmed to be implemented for FY 2017 based on the approved LGU budget, in compliance with Item 4.1.2 of the same DBM Budget Circular, in order to substantiate the grant of CNA Incentive to its officials and employees.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Restore back the unliquidated amount of cash advances for periods from 2013 to 2014 pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-385 dated October 1, 1992.
b. Submit copies of transmittal letters duly “Received” by the ICFAU authorized staff for cash advances covering periods from 2015 to 2017 in support to the liquidations recorded in December 2017.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant:
a. Strictly comply with the documentary requirements for the transfer of funds to barangay as implementing agencies of government program and projects pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
b. Record fund transfers to barangays under Due from LGUs account instead of Donation or Subsidy to Local Government Units for easy monitoring of all fund transfers to barangays and their liquidations.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Municipality Accountant:
a. Conduct immediate investigation on the irreconcilable discrepancies at an earlier time to resolve the differences so that corrections will be promptly incorporated in the books.
b. Reconcile their records at least quarterly, as provided in Section 181(c) of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume I, to avoid the accumulation of discrepancies and so that necessary adjustments will be effected immediately in order to reflect correct and reliable Cash – Local Treasury balance.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Government of Murcia fully institutionalize the MDRRM Office, assign a full time MDRRM Officer and hire three staff (full time) responsible for administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning pursuant to Section 12 of RA 10121.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality Government:
1. Intensify the “no segregation, no collection policy” among the residents of the Municipality.
2. Enforce strict implementation of the provisions of Sections 21 and 22 of RA 9003 on the mandatory segregation of solid wastes and carry out the following:
a. Strengthen Information and Education Campaign activities like information dissemination relative to solid waste management.
b. Establish close coordination with barangay officials to ensure that efforts to improve the SWM will reach the constituents at the barangay level.
c. Develop a strategic scheme, like providing incentives, for the purpose of encouraging constituents or enterprises to develop or undertake responsible and effective solid waste management.
d. Impose fines and penalties for any offense made or violation of Section 48 of RA 9003.
3. Provide collection and transport schedules and require the use of separate trucks for specific types of wastes as required by Section 24 of RA 9003.
4. Ensure the establishment or maintenance of MRF in all barangays pursuant to Section 32 of RA 9003.
5. Implement the closure of the open dump, construct and operate a sanitary landfill as a final disposal site for solid wastes to ensure protection of public health and the environment.

Recommendation:
We recommended that only transactions/expenses related to programs/projects/activities embodied in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 shall be charged against the 20% Development Fund in order to achieve the objectives of establishing the Developmental Fund.
Further, we recommend that the Municipality enforce the full implementation of the programs, projects and activities embodied in the annual development plan so that a positive impact on a wider scale can be felt in the locality.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the members of the Local School Board, in the preparation of the School Board budget should give priority to programs, activities and projects enumerated in Section 363(c) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Local Government Code and must utilize the proceeds of the additional one percent Special Education Fund to such purpose prescribed by law for which SEF can be disposed of.
We recommended that the School Board refrain from utilizing the SEF fund for payment of expenses that are not priority program, projects and activities in compliance to section 363(c) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Local Government Code as well as in no.4 of DEPED, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality fast track the implementation of all BuB and ADM projects funded from the national government agencies to ensure that the desired benefits/services could be delivered to the Municipality’s constituents on time and could address the needs of the poor sectors of the Municipality.
We also recommended that the Project Monitoring Committee of Local Poverty Reduction Action Team (LPRAT) or designated focal person continue to monitor the implementation of all BuB projects pursuant to the guidelines under DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 7 dated November 3, 2015.

The GSO-Designate conduct a yearly physical count of all the agency’s properties and submit a complete Inventory Report, which shall include the actual status of the assets whether it is unserviceable, scrap or already junk, not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with the requirement of Section 124 of the NGAS Manual and Section 490 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual. b. The GSO-Designate reconcile the Inventory Report with the accounting records to reflect the real and actual cost of properties. Likewise, properties with no available historical records should be appraised/revalued by an Appraisal Committee created by the Local Chief Executive.

           124 of the  NGAS  Manual  and  Section  490  of  the  Government
           Accounting and Auditing Manual.

observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive will direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality and to prepare the required Report on the Physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and to submit such report to the Auditor on/or before due date.
observations.reason: Inventory Committee was created but have not conducted actual and physical count.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepares the monthly bank reconciliation statement and cause the timely submission of the same in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant will see to it that Bank Reconciliation Statements will be submitted within the required period.
observations.reason: Bank Reconciliation Statements for all Funds as of December 31, 2014 are already available as of balance sheet date but were not yet officially transmitted to the Auditor. Monthly Bank Reconciliation were prepared on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Accountant and the LDRRMO comply in the preparation and submission of the monthly report of sources and utilization of the LDRRMF in compliance with Section 5.1.5.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief executive had instructed the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office - In charge of Office to submit monthly report on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF thru the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) and Local Development Council (LDC) to the office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Only Annual Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer/ Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting cash advances to officers and employees with outstanding cash advances to prevent the accumulation of cash advances. Adhere strictly to the policy that no additional cash advances shall be granted to officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances. Only cash advance on travel will be allowed to elective officials as mandated by Section 4.1.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002 hence, assign a regular employee to handle cash advances for Confidential/Intelligence purposes. We further recommend that the agency hasten the submission of requirements to the COA Head Office necessary for the issuance of credit notice in order to settle the outstanding cash advances for Confidential and Intelligence Expenses.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant see to it that no additional cash advance is granted when there is unliquidated cash advance. Unliquidated cash advances of prior years are deducted from the payroll of the concerned employee.
observations.reason: Material portion of the unliquidated Cash Advance as of December 31, 2014 comprises of Confidential Fund whose liquidation were already sent to COA Central Office but were not yet issued credit advise.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the agency adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 100 of RA 7160 and DECS, DBM, DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 in the utilization/disposition of the SEF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality of Murcia through its Local School Board will prioritize the expenses provided under Section 100(c) of RA 7160 and DECS, DBM, DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 1998 on the utilization of Special Education Fund.
observations.reason: Management reasoned out that the disbursements are covered by an approved budget of the Local School Board.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer update entries in the cashbook and reconcile the Cash in Vault account with the accounting records monthly to avoid discrepancies.
observations.managementAction: There is an on-going reconciliation between the cashbook of the OIC-Municipal Treasurer with that of the Accounting records.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accounting Department should exert extra effort to update the submission of the required financial reports.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant will exert extra effort to comply with the provisions of Section 70 of the Manual on the new Government Accounting System Volume I and likewise with Section 72 of the same manual. Rest assured 2015 financial reports will be submitted on time.
observations.reason: Monthly Trial Balances were not submitted on time due to the delay also of the submission of documents from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer such as Disbursement Vouchers, RCDs and the liquidation of cash advances made by the disbursing officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that only transactions related to programs/projects/activities embodied in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 be charged to the 20% Development Fund in order to achieve the objectives of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive will ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environment outcomes. Only programs/projects/activities embodied in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-11 will be charged to the 20% Development Fund in order to achieve the objectives of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Management implemented what was covered by the approved 20% Development Fund Budget.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Accounting Department should exert extra effort to update the submission of the required financial reports.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to update the submission of the said reports.
observations.reason: Voluminous transactions with few personnel.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% development fund be strictly followed.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO and Municipal Budget Offices were instructed to strictly adhere with the provision of DILG-DBM JMC 2011-1.
observations.reason: Management implemented what was covered by the approved 20% Development Fund Budget.

observations.recommendation: Direct the accountable officers concerned to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period, otherwise sanctions should be imposed for failure to render the accounts in accordance with Section 16 of E.O. No. 248, as amended by E.O. No. 298.
observations.managementAction: Some accountable officers settled their cash advances partially thru salary deduction.
observations.reason: Additional cash advances were granted despite non-liquidation of previous availments.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive should ensure periodic inventory of all PPE as well as prompt submission of the required Report of Physical Count of Properties, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive will direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality and to prepare the required Report on the Physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and to submit such report to the Auditor on/or before due date.
observations.reason: Inventory Committee was created but have not conducted actual and physical count.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Treasurer to restitute the amount of P4,297,312.57 and file appropriate charges against him, if warranted, for the incurrence of the shortage.
observations.managementAction: The agency is now effecting the withholding of the salary of Mr. Conte monthly less his mandatory deductions in restitution for his shortage. He is now detailed at the Office of the Provincial Treasurer.
observations.reason: The full amount of the shortage was not yet restituted.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor should require the Assistant Municipal Treasurer to restitute the amount and appropriate charges shall be filed against her, if warranted, for the incurrence of the shortage.
observations.managementAction: Mrs. de Isidro’s salary is being withheld in restitution of her shortage. She is now detailed with the Office of the Provincial Treasurer.
observations.reason: The full amount of the shortage was not yet restituted.

observations.recommendation: Direct the accountable officers concerned to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period, otherwise sanction will be imposed in accordance with Section 16 of E.O. No. 248 as amended.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were issued to employees with outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: The documents to support the liquidation of cash advances were no longer available.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall submit on time the disbursement vouchers to expedite the post audit work to ensure prompt issuances of Certificate of Settlement and Balances. Likewise, timely submission of the financial reports will guide management in making sound decision regarding financial matters of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to update the submission of reports and preparation of bank reconciliation statements.
observations.reason: Submission of supporting documents were delayed resulting in the delayed submission of DVs to COA. BRS were not prepared in the prior years’.
